content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
428 J Street
• Sacramento. CA 95812-0807
(916)322-5660
Fax(916)322-08$6
August 25, 1998
Richard E. Archibald
Senior Deputy City Attorney
City of Sacramento
980 Ninth Street, Tenth Floor
Sacramento, California 95814-2736
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-98-1 84
Dear Mr. Archibald:
This letter is in response to your request for informal advice’ regarding the provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
2
QUESTION
May Councilmember Yee participate in discussions and decisions regarding a
development project which in the past he abstained from participation due to a potential material
financial effect on an individual Or entity which at that time, had been a source of income to
Councilmember Yee within the prior twelve months?
CONCLUSION
Unless Councilmember Yee has some other disqualifying interest, the fact that it is
foreseeable that a former source of income may be materially financially affected by a
governmental decision, will not disqualify him from participating in the decision.
FACTS
Mr. Jimmie R. Yee is a councilmember for the City of Sacramento, District 4. A
development project which came before the city council some time ago had the potential to have
a material financial effect on an individual or entity which had been a source of income to
Councilmember Yee within twelve months of the decision. As a result, Mr. Yee declared a
conflict of interest and abstained from participation in the matter. The matter was ultimately
Informal advice does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)
2 Government Code Sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, Sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. 1-98-184
Page 2
continued for a lengthy period of time. The matter is now likely to come back to the council
soon for further consideration and possible action. The former source of income, which resulted
in Mr. Yee’s declaration of a conflict of interest, is no longer a source of income to Mr. Yee and
has not been for a number of years.
ANALYSIS
Government Code Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, participating in
making, or in any way attempting to use his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a fmancial interest. Section 87103
provides that an official has a financial interest if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decisions
will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on
any one of a number of enumerated interests, including:
“(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans
by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of
business on terms available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifiy dollars ($250) or more in
value provided to, received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.”
The facts state that it is foreseeable that the decisions regarding a development project,
which is anticipated to come before the city council, will have a material financial effect on an
individual or entity that was a source of income of $250 or more to Councilmember Yee in the
past. However, Councilmember Yee has not received any income from this source for a number
nfvears. Under the clear definition of the statute, this is not an economic interest within the
meaning of Section 87100, and Councilmember Yee does not have to disqualify himself from
participating in decisions regarding this development project should this matter come before the
city council.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
Q
eral Counsel
:
Amy Holloway /
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May Councilmember Yee participate in discussions and decisions regarding a
development project which in the past he abstained from participation due to a potential material
financial effect on an individual Or entity which at that time, had been a source of income to
Councilmember Yee within the prior twelve months?

CONCLUSION: Unless Councilmember Yee has some other disqualifying interest, the fact that it is
foreseeable that a former source of income may be materially financially affected by a
governmental decision, will not disqualify him from participating in the decision.
